commands.author: Takhoffman
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `/clawsweeper build override` parsing, refusal copy, hard/soft blocker classification, override metadata in issue implementation jobs, operations docs, and focused parser/intake/router tests.

Reproducibility: yes. The blocking issues are source-reproducible from the PR head: existing-job rewrites return as `existing`, linked PR detection only scans issue-side text, and the security classifier omits issue body text.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can fix the remaining dispatch-order and classifier evidence gaps with focused regression coverage on the PR branch.

target.title: feat: add issue build override
target.body:
## Summary

- add `/clawsweeper build override` parsing and override metadata for issue implementation jobs
- classify issue implementation blockers as soft or hard so overrides either attempt one bounded PR or produce a safe non-code handoff
- update refusal messaging, operations docs, and focused parser/intake/router tests
- hard override jobs now validate as non-code handoffs by blocking `fix`, `raise_pr`, `close`, and `merge`; existing issue implementation jobs are rewritten when an override is requested

## Validation

- `pnpm run build`
- `pnpm run build:repair`
- `pnpm exec node --test test/repair/comment-router-core.test.ts test/repair/issue-implementation-intake.test.ts`
- `pnpm run check`

## Override proof

Local live proof command after `build:repair` exercised the soft/hard override paths and hard job frontmatter:

```json
{
  "soft": {
    "status": "override_queued_for_repair",
    "shouldRepair": true,
    "blockerClass": "soft",
    "operatorOverride": true
  },
  "hard": {
    "status": "override_handoff",
    "shouldRepair": true,
    "blockerClass": "hard",
    "operatorOverride": true
  },
  "hardJob": {
    "validateErrors": [],
    "allowedActions": ["comment", "label"],
    "blockedActions": ["fix", "raise_pr", "close", "merge"],
    "allowFixPr": false,
    "containsFixArtifactInstruction": false,
    "containsHandoffInstruction": true
  }
}
```

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/issues/200
target.author: takhoffman

commands.author: Takhoffman

target.title: feat: add visualize commands
target.body:
## Summary
- add @clawsweeper visualize command parsing and dispatch through the read-only assist lane
- generate marker-backed visual brief comments that update in place by item/lens/head
- filter ClawSweeper visual comments out of later review context
- document standard glyph meanings for visual briefs and when to use them

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/issues/202

Note: this is a command/comment slice and intentionally does not claim to close the full issue 202 review-hint acceptance matrix.

## ClawSweeper follow-up
- fixed visual footer ordering so metadata appears before the generated brief and the maintainer ruling footer remains final
- fixed workflow_dispatch fallback so manual mode/lens inputs are honored
- tightened unknown value handling for type-aware lint
- added explicit visual glyph guidance for expected, broken, risk, bug path, trust boundary, disk, runtime, and maintainer judgment markers

## Real behavior proof
- Ran the real assist workflow on this PR branch with mode=visual and lens=state at current head 7e924b66922a3f50d404cd46d155ee487ceb69b7: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/actions/runs/26430248898
- The workflow posted a marker-backed visual comment for PR 204 at that head: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/pull/204#issuecomment-4539684232
- The visual output includes glyph usage and a legend where useful.

## Validation
- node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.json --pretty false
- node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.repair.json --pretty false
- node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.dashboard.json --pretty false
- node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check .github/workflows/assist.yml src/clawsweeper.ts src/repair/comment-router-core.ts src/repair/comment-router.ts test/clawsweeper.test.ts test/repair/comment-router-core.test.ts
- node --test test/repair/comment-router-core.test.ts --test-name-pattern visualize
- node --test test/clawsweeper.test.ts --test-name-pattern "review context comment filter"
- node_modules/.bin/oxlint src --ignore-pattern "src/repair/**" --tsconfig tsconfig.json --type-aware --deny-warnings --report-unused-disable-directives -D correctness
